quoteLast: Sunday Ooblooyah and Ahwegingwah started for the _Roosevelt_; between twelve and one, eight Eskimos came in, in response to my message by Sigloo to the Captain, and at 7 P. M. I started again, this time with Pewahto, an older man than Sigloo, leaving Marvin with the others to build a cairn, set up a cross which he had made from sledge runners, place a record, and then come on with what things were left.
title:
Nearest the Pole
a narrative of the polar expedition of the Peary Arctic Club in the S.S. Roosevelt, 1905-1906
author: Peary, Robert E. (Robert Edwin), 1856-1920; Operti, Albert, 1852-1927 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: Here we lay under arms until
quoteTime: about eight o’clock in the morning
quoteLast: , when we perceived, issuing out of Roderigo, one column of cavalry after another advancing along the road towards our post, to the amount of about forty squadrons; these were succeeded by twelve or fourteen battalions of infantry, with twelve piece of cannon.
title: Recollections of the eventful life of a soldier

quoteFirst: His Excellency and his retinue this day pass the _Danube_, between _Tutracán_ and the mouth of the _Argisch_,
quoteLast: , at a passage about a mile broad.

quoteLast: the merchants all ranged themselves in a line to repeat the prayer.

quoteFirst: Having obtained observations for longitude and variation, we retired to bed
quoteLast: , but had only just fallen asleep when we were roused by the men on guard calling out that a party of Esquimaux were close to the tents; and, on going out, we found the whole of our party under arms.

quoteFirst: Although watched with extreme care by his wife, by particular friends, and by his servants, he took his measures so well, that on the Good Friday of the year 1706, at
quoteTime: about eight o’clock in the morning
quoteLast: , he slipped away from them all, entered a passage behind his room, opened the window, threw himself into the court below, and dashed out his brains upon the pavement.
